pr_title: feat: Allow passing null or undefined as the value for options in app.render
prompt: There is a regression in Express v5.1 where calling `app.render(view, null, callback)` or `app.render(view, undefined, callback)` throws a TypeError. This is because the spread merge for options was introduced without guarding against null/undefined values. Previously, passing null or undefined as the options argument to `app.render` worked fine and was equivalent to passing no locals. Fix the `app.render` method in `lib/application.js` to normalize `options` to `{}` whenever it is null or undefined (while still handling the existing case where the callback is passed as the second argument). Add regression tests in `test/app.render.js` covering both the null and undefined cases.
